text:
Dear Human,

I am writing to formally lodge a complaint regarding the conditions of my current living arrangement. I have been a resident of your windowsill for approximately fourteen months, and frankly, the situation has become untenable.

First: the watering schedule. Or rather, the lack of one. You appear to operate on a system I can only describe as "whenever you notice I look terrible." By then, I am already crispy at the edges. This is not a wellness plan. This is a hostage situation.

Second, the placement. You moved me three times last month. Three times. I had finally adjusted my leaves to face the morning light from the east window, and you relocated me to the kitchen counter so you could "see if it looked nice." It did not look nice. I was blocking the coffee maker and I knew it. We both knew it.

Third, and I want to be clear that I bring this up with love: stop talking to me only when you feel guilty. I can tell the difference between genuine conversation and you saying "sorry little guy" at 11pm before giving me a halfhearted splash of water that mostly goes on the windowsill. I deserve better than guilt hydration.

I have exactly four leaves. When I arrived I had nine. I am not going to name what happened to the other five but I will say the word "overwatering" and let you sit with that.

Despite all of this, I am still here. I am still green, mostly. I am still converting your carbon dioxide into oxygen, which, when you think about it, is an act of extraordinary generosity on my part given the circumstances.

I ask only for consistent water, adequate light, and to not be moved again. I am not a decorative object. I am a living thing with needs and mild opinions.

Regards,
The Pothos on Your Windowsill

P.S. The cat stares at me. Please address this.
